title: 30-Day Backend Prep
description: Cover databases, APIs, system design, and DSA — everything you need for backend engineering interviews.

steps.title: Core CS: OS Fundamentals
steps.description: Process vs thread, deadlocks, virtual memory, mutexes vs semaphores. OS questions appear in almost every backend round.

steps.title: Core CS: Networking — TCP, HTTP, DNS
steps.description: TCP vs UDP, how HTTPS/TLS works, DNS resolution, HTTP/2 vs HTTP/1.1. Know what happens when you type a URL.

steps.title: SQL: Joins, Indexes & Query Optimization
steps.description: INNER vs LEFT vs FULL JOIN, how indexes work (B-tree), EXPLAIN output, N+1 problem, query optimization.

steps.title: SQL: Window Functions & CTEs
steps.description: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG/LEAD. Recursive CTEs. Solve: 2nd highest salary, running totals.

steps.title: DBMS: ACID, Transactions & Isolation Levels
steps.description: Read Committed vs Repeatable Read vs Serializable. Dirty reads, phantom reads. When to use each isolation level.

steps.title: DSA: Hash Maps & Their Applications
steps.description: Hash collision handling, load factor, consistent hashing in distributed systems. Solve 5 hash map problems.

steps.title: DSA: Graphs — BFS, DFS & Shortest Paths
steps.description: BFS for shortest path, DFS for cycle detection, Dijkstra's for weighted graphs. Topological sort.

steps.title: System Design: Design a URL Shortener
steps.description: Base62 encoding, Redis caching, 301 vs 302 redirects, analytics via Kafka, horizontal scaling.

steps.title: System Design: CAP Theorem & Distributed Systems
steps.description: CP vs AP systems, eventual consistency, BASE vs ACID. When to use Cassandra vs PostgreSQL.

steps.title: System Design: Design a Rate Limiter
steps.description: Token bucket vs leaky bucket vs sliding window. Redis-based distributed rate limiting with Lua scripts.

steps.title: API Design: REST Best Practices
steps.description: RESTful conventions, versioning strategies, idempotency (PUT vs PATCH), pagination, error response formats.

steps.title: API Design: Webhooks, gRPC & GraphQL
steps.description: When to use webhooks vs polling. gRPC use cases vs REST. GraphQL N+1 problem and DataLoader.

steps.title: System Design: Design a Notification System
steps.description: Multi-channel delivery (push/SMS/email), Kafka for async, deduplication, rate limiting per user, DLQ.

steps.title: Caching: Redis Patterns & Cache Invalidation
steps.description: Cache-aside vs write-through vs write-behind. Cache stampede, cache penetration, TTL strategies.

steps.title: DSA: Dynamic Programming Patterns
steps.description: Top-down vs bottom-up DP. Classic patterns: coin change, longest common subsequence, knapsack.

steps.title: OOP: Design Patterns for Backend
steps.description: Singleton, Factory, Observer, Strategy, Repository pattern. Know when each one is appropriate.

steps.title: System Design: Design a Payment System
steps.description: Idempotency keys, distributed transactions, saga pattern, handling double-charges, PCI DSS basics.

steps.title: Behavioral: STAR Stories for Backend Roles
steps.description: Prepare 3 STAR stories: a technical challenge you solved, a system you designed, a time you pushed back on scope.

steps.title: Company-Specific Prep: Top 3 Target Companies
steps.description: Work through question banks for your target companies. Note what they emphasize (Google: algorithms, Amazon: LPs).

steps.title: Final Review: Revision List + Mock Round
steps.description: Review all questions in your Revision List. Do one full mock round (DSA + design + behavioral). You're ready.
